---
description: 'Enforce SEO compliance checks on every change to Hugo templates, layouts, partials, content files, or migration scripts that affect URL structure, canonical tags, metadata, sitemap, robots.txt, or structured data'
applyTo: 'layouts/**,content/**,hugo.toml,static/robots.txt,scripts/**'
---

# SEO Compliance Gate

## Mandatory Pre-Check

Before modifying any file matched by `applyTo`, ask: **Does this change affect any of the following?**

1. URL structure or routing (front matter `url`, `aliases`, `permalinks` config, taxonomy paths)
2. Canonical tags or `<link rel="canonical">` output
3. Meta title or meta description generation
4. Sitemap output (`sitemap.xml` inclusion, exclusion, or URL shape)
5. `robots.txt` directives
6. Open Graph or Twitter card tags
7. JSON-LD structured data blocks
8. Redirect pages generated by Hugo aliases

If **yes to any**, apply the full SEO Compliance Checklist below before completing the change.

## SEO Compliance Checklist

### URL Safety
- [ ] Any new or changed `url` front matter value is lowercase, starts with `/`, ends with `/`, contains only `a-z 0-9 - /`
- [ ] No duplicate `url` values exist across content files (run collision check: `scripts/validate-frontmatter.js`)
- [ ] Any new `aliases` value exists in `migration/url-manifest.json`
- [ ] No redirect chains introduced: source path resolves in one hop to final destination
- [ ] Any change to `permalinks` in `hugo.toml` triggers a full URL parity check run

### Canonical Signals
- [ ] Canonical tag defaults to `.Permalink` — override only with absolute `https://www.rhino-inquisitor.com/...` URL
- [ ] Canonical tag URL matches sitemap URL for the same page
- [ ] Internal links point to the canonical `www` variant — not apex, not `http`
- [ ] Alias/redirect pages are excluded from `sitemap.xml` (verify Hugo default holds)

### Metadata
- [ ] Every indexable page has a unique `title` (max 60 chars recommended)
- [ ] Every indexable page has a `description` (120–155 chars)
- [ ] `draft: true` pages are excluded from sitemap and do not appear in production build output

### Structured Data
- [ ] JSON-LD changes validated with [Google Rich Results Test](https://search.google.com/test/rich-results)
- [ ] All string values in JSON-LD use Hugo `jsonify` function
- [ ] `datePublished` and `dateModified` use ISO 8601 format with timezone
- [ ] `BlogPosting` emitted only on article-type templates, not on list/taxonomy pages

### Redirect Safety
- [ ] No mass redirect of unrelated URLs to homepage
- [ ] No soft-404 risk: retired URLs with organic traffic map to nearest relevant content
- [ ] Staging or preview environments have `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">` — **not** only `robots.txt Disallow`

### robots.txt
- [ ] `robots.txt` changes do not accidentally block crawling of production content
- [ ] `Sitemap:` directive points to `https://www.rhino-inquisitor.com/sitemap.xml`
- [ ] `robots.txt` is never used as the sole mechanism for de-indexing — pair with `noindex` meta tags

## When This Is Not Required

- Purely editorial content changes (e.g., fixing a typo in body text with no front matter or URL changes)
- CSS, image, or font asset changes with no URL structure implications
- Local development configuration that does not affect production build output

## Escalation

If a change introduces a URL structure alteration affecting >5% of indexed URLs, escalate to SEO owner before merging. This triggers the edge redirect architecture decision (Phase 2 contract).
